claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances and the child or children you're enquiring about.

Let's start with the basics:

**How many children are you enquiring about, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! Thanks for helping me with this. I'm enquiring about one child - my daughter Alex. She's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's lovely to meet you, and I'm happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Is Alex living with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

**Are you responsible for Alex?** This means you're the person who is mainly looking after her day-to-day needs, such as food, clothing, and accommodation.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I am. I look after her day-to-day - all her food, clothes, and everything she needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Lovely, thank you for confirming that.

**Do you live in the UK?** And is Alex also living in the UK with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - we're in Manchester. Alex lives here with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Wonderful, thank you. Just a couple more questions to check your eligibility.

**Are you receiving any public funds, or do you have any immigration restrictions that might affect your right to claim benefits?** For example, do you have "No recourse to public funds" written on your visa or immigration status documents, or are you in the UK on a visa that limits your access to benefits?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have any restrictions like that. I'm able to claim benefits.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

One last question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child at any one time.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for all that information. Based on what you've told me, I have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me confirm what you've shared:
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
- Alex lives with you in the UK (Manchester)
- You are responsible for her day-to-day care
- You have no immigration restrictions affecting your right to claim benefits
- No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why you qualify:

✅ **Alex is under 16** – She's 8 years old, which meets the age requirement.

✅ **You live in the UK** – You're based in Manchester.

✅ **Alex lives with you** – She resides at your home.

✅ **You're responsible for Alex** – You look after her day-to-day needs including food, clothes, and accommodation.

✅ **No immigration restrictions** – You have the right to claim benefits.

✅ **No duplicate claims** – No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

You can apply for Child Benefit online or by post. If you have any other questions, please feel free to ask!

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement. She lives with her parent in the UK (Manchester), so the residency requirement is met. The claimant is responsible for Alex's day-to-day needs including food, clothes, and accommodation, which satisfies the 'responsible for a child' requirement. The claimant has no immigration restrictions preventing them from claiming benefits. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria for Child Benefit are therefore satisfied.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit. They live in the UK, are responsible for one child (Alex, aged 8) who lives with them, have no immigration restrictions on claiming benefits, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement. She lives with her parent in the UK (Manchester), so the residency requirement is met. The claimant is responsible for Alex's day-to-day needs including food, clothes, and accommodation, which satisfies the 'responsible for a child' requirement. The claimant has no immigration restrictions preventing them from claiming benefits. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria for Child Benefit are therefore satisfied.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit. They live in the UK, are responsible for one child (Alex, aged 8) who lives with them, have no immigration restrictions on claiming benefits, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.
